title: Prototype Pollution
principle: Never let attacker-controlled keys reach an object's prototype. Reject or skip the keys __proto__, constructor, and prototype when merging, cloning, or setting properties from untrusted data. Prefer structures without a prototype (Map, or Object.create(null)) and use vetted, patched merge libraries.

snippets.library: a Map or null-prototype object, and a guarded deep-merge
snippets.vulnerable: function merge(t, s){ for (const k in s){ if (typeof s[k]==='object') merge(t[k]=t[k]||{}, s[k]); else t[k]=s[k]; } }
snippets.secure:
function merge(t, s){ for (const k in s){ if (k==='__proto__'||k==='constructor'||k==='prototype') continue; if (s[k]&&typeof s[k]==='object'){ t[k]=t[k]||{}; merge(t[k], s[k]); } else t[k]=s[k]; } }
// or store untrusted maps in `new Map()` / `Object.create(null)`
snippets.note: The classic sink is a recursive merge/set that follows __proto__. Skip those keys, or use a lodash version >= 4.17.21 and Object.freeze(Object.prototype) as defense in depth.

references.title: OWASP Prototype Pollution Prevention Cheat Sheet
